reasoning: In April 2025, following the appointment of Robert F. Kennedy Jr. as Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS), an official directive was issued to end the federal recommendation for water fluoridation. A Congressional Research Service (CRS) report dated May 16, 2025, explicitly confirms that the Secretary 'directed the end of its water fluoridation recommendation' in April 2025. Given that the CDC is an agency within HHS, it is highly likely that its official recommendation (previously 0.7 mg/L) will be removed, retracted, or replaced with a 'no recommendation' stance by July 1, 2025. Since the resolution criteria specify that 'no recommendation' or pulling the page offline results in a 'No' resolution, and given the high-level political priority of this change, the probability of the recommendation still standing on July 1 is very low.
